root_text.sn45.175:1.3: Kāmarāgānusayo, paṭighānusayo, diṭṭhānusayo, vicikicchānusayo, mānānusayo, bhavarāgānusayo, avijjānusayo—
root_text.sn45.175:1.5: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, sattannaṁ anusayānaṁ abhiññāya pariññāya parikkhayāya pahānāya …pe… ayaṁ ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo bhāvetabbo”ti.

translation_text.sn45.175:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these seven underlying tendencies.
translation_text.sn45.175:1.3: The underlying tendencies of sensual desire, repulsion, views, doubt, conceit, desire for continued existence, and ignorance.
translation_text.sn45.175:1.4: These are the seven underlying tendencies.
translation_text.sn45.175:1.5: The noble eightfold path should be developed for the direct knowledge, complete understanding, finishing, and giving up of these seven underlying tendencies.”
